transcription: <Speaker1>: also in Johor, a twenty three year old woman died after a Proton Myvi car driven by her husband collided with another car at kilometre forty seven of Jalan Johor Bahru Mersing near Mawar Kota Tinggi. The accident occurred at eleven o two am, which also injured her seven month old baby and her husband. Kota Tinggi district police chief Superintendent Ashmon Bajah said the woman identified as Izzati Mustafa. <Speaker1>: Mustafa died at the Kota Tngi Hospital, her daughter Afiah Medina and husband twenty six year old Muhammad Ridzwan Nordien and the driver of the other car were injured and were being treated at the Kota Tnggi Hospital. Initial investigations found that the other car a Proton Persona lost control and subsequently entered the opposite lane and collided with a Prodom Mvy. The case is being investigated under the Road Transport Act One Nine Eighty Seven.

transcription: <Speaker1>: three eateries, including one famed for its durian ice cream were ordered to shut down following a joint inspection by the Penang Island City Council and State Health Department. About twenty officers inspected six eateries along Penang Road. A total of ten compounds ranging from one hundred ringgit. <Speaker1>: from one hundred ringgit to two hundred fifty ringgit were also issued during the health inspection, M B P P senior health officer Goh Eng Choon said the inspection aims to heighten the level of cleanliness of eateries in Penang. Goh said that inspections will be carried out weekly instead of fortnightly.

transcription: <Speaker1>: heavy rain over the last forty eight hours triggered landslides on Mount Kinabalu in Kundasang, Ranau, some one hundred kilometres from Kota Kinabalu early Sunday. The first incident is believed to have occurred at about one twenty am, and a mud flood on one side of the mountain occurred at five am. Sabah Parks director Dr. Maklarin Lakim said. <Speaker1>: Akeem said the landslide occurred far from the summit trail and did not involve any accident involving people or damage to infrastructure. Ranau district authorities were monitoring the landslide debris that had fallen into the Mesilau River that flows into the Liwagu River, where the district's water intake and treatment plant is located.
